name: outgrowing-god
description: Richard Dawkins' 'Outgrowing God: A Beginner's Guide' — a clear, accessible argument against religion written for young readers. Part 1: why gods are myths, the unreliability of scripture, and that morality doesn't require God. Part 2: evolution as the real explanation for life's complexity, the illusion of design, and the evolution of religion and morality.
useCase: Understanding the arguments for atheism, why evolution disproves intelligent design, why scripture is unreliable, how morality can exist without religion, and how to think critically about religious claims.
knownRisks: Written for young readers — may oversimplify complex issues
Dawkins is a polemicist, not an impartial scholar
Some readers will find the tone dismissive of religious beliefs
The evolution section assumes acceptance of Darwinian theory
